title: Creator Marketing Manager
description:
Established in 2004, OLIVER is the world’s first and only specialist in designing, building, and running bespoke in-house agencies and marketing ecosystems for brands. We partner with over 300 clients in 40+ countries and counting. Our unique model drives creativity and efficiency, allowing us to deliver tailored solutions that resonate deeply with audiences.

As a part of The Brandtech Group, we're at the forefront of leveraging cutting-edge AI technology to revolutionise how we create and deliver work. Our AI solutions enhance efficiency, spark creativity, and drive insightful decision-making, empowering our teams to produce innovative and impactful results.

Role: Creator Marketing Manager

Location: Gurgaon, India

About the role:

As the Creator Marketing Manager for Google India’s social channels, you are the voice of Google to millions of Indians. You won't just be "writing copy"—you’ll be building the narrative of how India uses technology to dream, learn, and grow. From high-production brand campaigns to real-time "trend-jacking" and collaborating with India’s top creators, you will ensure Google India remains the most helpful and loved brand on the Indian internet.

What you will be doing:

  • Building the creator infrastructure for the team - identifying, brand safety checks, briefing - end to end process.
  • The Voice of Google India: Define and execute the creative tone for Google India across YouTube Shorts, Instagram, X, and more. You’ll turn "product features" into "cultural moments."
  • Social Storytelling: Lead the end-to-end creation of social-first content—from scripting "day in the life" creator videos to conceptualizing interactive stories about Google Maps or Search trends.
  • Trend-Jacking & Real-Time Content: Monitor the Indian cultural pulse (Cricket, Bollywood, exams, festivals) and find authentic ways for Google to join the conversation.
  • Creator Collaboration: Partner with India's leading influencers and creators to co-create content that feels like a natural part of their feed, not a forced ad.
  • Creative Directing Agencies: Lead and inspire our agency partners, ensuring their output is "social-native" and meets Google’s high bar for craft and empathy.
  • Stakeholder Navigation: Work with global product teams and local marketing leads to translate complex business goals into simple, delightful social content.
  • Budget & Production Ownership: Manage the lifecycle of social productions, from annual agency contracts to specific campaign budgets.

What you need to be great in this role:

  • 8+ years of experience in digital marketing, social media agencies, or content platform preferably in the creator space
  • A portfolio that showcases social-first thinking (not just print ads or TVCs).
  • Experience managing large-scale brand social handles.
  • Exceptional writing skills in English (fluency in Hindi or other Indian languages is a massive plus).
  • Platform Mastery: Deep expertise in YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels ecosystems.
  • The "Script-to-Screen" Skill: Experience in directing or producing short-form video content.
  • Creator Network: Existing knowledge of or relationships within the Indian creator economy.
  • Analytical Mindset: The ability to look at a Google Trends dashboard or social analytics and say, "There’s a story here."
  • Deep understanding of the creator ecosystem
  • Cultural Intuition: You have a deep love for Indian internet culture and understand the nuances of different regions, languages, and behaviors.
  • The "Maker" Mindset: You aren’t afraid to pick up a phone to shoot a demo or jump into a rough edit to show a vision. You think in "hooks" and "retention."
  • Strategic Creativity: You don't just ideate for "likes"; you ideate to build brand love and solve user problems.
  • Collaboration Skills: You can present a bold, quirky idea to a room of corporate stakeholders and get them excited about it.
  • Operational Excellence: You can manage multiple workstreams (Always-on vs. Big Campaigns) without breaking a sweat.

Role: Product Marketing Manager

Location: Gurgaon, India

About the role:

As the Android Marketing team, our goal is simple: to bring the magic of premium Android Devices and experiences to our users, via focused GTM efforts including brand, product and retail marketing. As an Android Product Marketing Manager for Google in India, you will be responsible for building and establishing Android as the OS of choice. You will be part of a high performing team which will define product strategy using the right user insights and value propositions, leading marketing campaigns with partners in India and globally. You will work closely with our Android cross-functional teams, regional and global marketing teams in building the Android user strategy, executing high impact campaigns and analysis. You will also be working with external Retail, OEM and carrier partners to build a robust Android ecosystem. Finally, your work will also require you to work with multiple partners and agencies, both creative and media, and inspire them to co-create meaningful and high impact Social 1st campaigns.

At its core, marketing at Google starts with technology and ends with the user, bringing both together in unconventional ways. Our job is to demonstrate how Google's products solve core problems--from the everyday to the epic, from the mundane to the monumental.

Area Description:

Know the user. Know the magic. Connect the two. At its core, marketing at Google starts with technology and ends with the user, bringing both together in unconventional ways. Our job is to demonstrate how Google's products solve the world's problems--from the everyday to the epic, from the mundane to the monumental. And we approach marketing in a way that only Google can--changing the game, redefining the medium, making the user the priority, and ultimately, letting the technology speak for itself

What you will be doing:

  • Work with our Retail and OEM partners to drive adoption of Android through marketing initiatives
  • Be responsible for end-to-end retail marketing strategy and execution (online and offline) for Android premium, along with core product marketing bringing out the goodness of the platform in India
  • Strategize and execute POS initiatives and ATL campaigns (as needed) collaborating with multiple internal/ external partners and teams, to drive business goals
  • Develop integrated marketing campaigns to make Android cool: This includes narratives focused on our users and measuring the impact of our programs, and building every campaign with a social first angle.
  • Work cross-functionally with internal stakeholders across Google to evaluate the marketing opportunities from a strategic, financial and operational perspective. In addition to shaping creative, monitoring and evaluating performance, and sharing campaign learnings with relevant teams.
  • In addition to shaping creative, monitoring and evaluating performance, and sharing campaign learnings with relevant teams.

What you need to be great in this role:

  • BA/BS degree or equivalent practical experience
  • 6 to 9 years of work

Role: Social Marketing Insights & Strategy Manager (Social Media Analyst)

Location: Gurgaon, India

About the role:

As a Social Marketing Insights Manager, you will bridge the gap between product strategy and cultural resonance. You will be a part of the analytics, measurement and insights teams for Google India’s social channels, ensuring our brands (like Search, Pixel, and Gemini) connect authentically with audiences. You are a subject matter expert in social platforms and AI-powered analytics, responsible for turning complex data into actionable stories that shape product positioning, creative content, and business growth.

Key responsibilities:

  • Strategy & Insights: Lead the measurement framework for all Google product areas.
  • Translate complex social data into concise narratives for cross-functional stakeholders (Product, Sales, Creative, and Legal).
  • Adept at and comfortable with building AI-first workflows to harness the power of the internet.
  • AI-Powered Listening: Build and manage real-time, AI-enabled social listening workflows to monitor brand sentiment, competitive landscapes, and emerging cultural trends.
  • Strategic Consulting: Act as an internal consultant for Product Marketing Managers (PMMs), framing business questions into investigative social studies and brand audits.
  • Creative Collaboration: Partner with editorial and creative teams to close the loop between data and content, using performance insights to modernize Google’s organic and paid social approach.
  • Agency & Playbook Management: Lead social agency relationships and vendors. Develop a "Lighthouse" playbook for measurement and insights to be used across regional teams.

What you will be doing:

  • Set KPIs and create comprehensive reports (weekly/monthly/campaign-specific) that visualize data and drive decision-making.
  • Create advanced search queries for social listening to uncover conversation drivers and audience habits.
  • Identify emerging formats and audience preferences to reach broader demographics.
  • Monitor and optimize the effectiveness of both organic and paid social initiatives.
  • Work on detailed reports for campaigns, all content working closely with agency

What you need to be great in this role:

  • Comfort building AI-first workflows in the measurements and insights space
  • Analytical Mastery: Strong ability to interpret digital media data, attribute results, and visualize insights in a way that informs business strategy.
  • Platform Expertise: Deep understanding of the mechanics and measurement nuances of YouTube, Instagram, X (Twitter), Facebook, and LinkedIn.
  • Technical Toolset: Proficiency in social listening and analytics tools (e.g., Brandwatch, Sprinklr, Google Analytics, Rival IQ, Facebook Insights, and Google Ads).
  • Marketing Fluency: Familiarity with the full product lifecycle, from positioning and competitive analysis to paid social optimization and influencer strategy.
  • Communication: Excellent ability to articulate "the why" behind the data to both technical and non-technical teams.
  • Passion for and inquisitive about AI and new technologies
  • Understanding

Role: Creator Marketing Manager

Location: Gurgaon, India

About the role:

As the Creator Marketing Manager for YouTube Shorts, you will be the driving force behind how we engage, recruit, and amplify the next generation of digital storytellers in India. This is a high-ownership role that sits at the intersection of brand strategy, data-driven experimentation, and cultural trend-spotting. You will lead the Go-To-Market (GTM) strategy, managing multi-million dollar campaigns and building a robust ecosystem of creators, labels, and partners.

What you will be doing:

1. Strategy & GTM Leadership:

  • Own the Mandate: Lead the end-to-end GTM strategy for YouTube Shorts in India, overseeing influencer campaign management from briefing to execution.
  • Partner Ecosystem: Orchestrate collaboration between creative agencies, media partners, talent labels, and internal business teams to drive product adoption.
  • Paid Media Integration: Partner with media agencies to implement high-impact paid strategies that scale reach and engagement.

2. Creator Recruitment & Optimization

  • Growth Engines: Develop and execute scalable strategies to recruit and optimize creator participation across YouTube products (Shorts, Shopping, Music).
  • Brand Stewardship: Ensure all agency-led executions align with global brand guidelines while maintaining local cultural relevance.
  • Trend Monitoring: Act as the internal "pulse" for the creator economy, identifying emerging trends and mitigating brand risks in real-time.

3. Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Global Alignment: Work seamlessly with APAC Marketing and Global Social teams to localize product launches and share best practices.
  • Stakeholder Management: Present campaign performance and strategic roadmaps to senior leadership with clarity and data-backed confidence.

4. Experimentation & Analysis

  • Test & Learn: Design and run robust marketing experiments to optimize creator selection and creative formats.
  • Data Synthesis: Translate complex campaign analytics into actionable insights, socializing "wins and fails" across the organization to inform future strategy.

What you need to be great in this role:

  • Experience: 9+ years in Marketing or Strategy (Agency or Client-side). A track record in the social media or creator ecosystem is a plus, but not a prerequisite for a fast learner.
  • Extreme Ownership: You are a self-starter who can navigate ambiguity and build a point of view from scratch.
  • Analytical Intuition: You are comfortable with data and social analytics tools, but you also possess the "creative gut" to know what content will go viral.
  • Project Mastery: Proven ability to manage complex budgets and multiple high-stakes campaigns simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.

Drive all Core asset workstreams across Mobility, Nest, Hearables & Wearables, including Packaging, Positioning (Product Lines, Copy Guide, Tech Specs, Naming), UI Screens, How-to Videos, and coordination with regional stakeholders on Partner Marketing Hub, and Gstore Site and CRM assets.

  • Drive creation of weekly Brand Team newsletter.

Sharing learnings & insights

Role: Events Marketing Specialist

Location: Gurgaon, India

About the role:

Developer Marketing is focused on driving the success of developer platforms, such as Android, by executing global, scalable programs, events and creative marketing campaigns to engage and delight developers. You need to have a passion for apps and games business models and understanding how developers use our APIs and tools to achieve business success.

The Events Specialist has to have a thorough knowledge of best practices in event management. This position requires a highly motivated individual with strong communication and customer service skills. In addition, the Event Specialist will focus on the needs and requirements of the Google products and programs and devise service-based solutions and best practices to exceed expectations.

What you will be doing:

Event Coordination Duties:

  • Ensure a great developer experience through High Quality Events.
  • Main point of contact for client events (external and internal)
  • Offer creative solutions for event execution
  • Coordinate event logistics including site selection, meeting room allocation, floor plan layout, directional signage development, material rentals, catering requirements, on-site event setup/teardown
  • Ensure accurate event tracking data
  • Provides post event metrics to determine process improvements for future programs

Vendor Management:

  • Source event vendors and create/maintain supplier list
  • Coordination of vendors as it relates to events including security, AV, F&B and so on.

Cross Functional

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to provide tailored marketing support and co-marketing plans to key partners
  • Collaborate with other developer facing & PR teams within various developer outreach initiatives

What you need to be great in this role:

  • Outstanding written and oral communication skills
  • Atleast BA/ BS degree in Marketing
  • 8-10 years experience in B2B event/program management
  • Ability to create briefs for event and marketing agencies (Creative, Events & Activations, Video production)
  • Strong analytical skills

YouTube is a cultural heartbeat in India—a platform where millions come to learn, connect, and be inspired. We are looking for a visionary lead to join the YouTube India social team through our staffing partner, Oliver. In this high-impact people management role, you will spearhead the YouTube master brand social workstreams, shaping how one of the world’s most significant brands resonates in a dynamic and growing market such as India

As the Lead for YouTube Brand & Social in India, you will be the strategic architect of YouTube’s personality across social platforms in India. description:
Established in 2004, OLIVER is the world’s first and only specialist in designing, building, and running bespoke in-house agencies and marketing ecosystems for brands. Role: Product Marketing Manager

Location: Bangalore, India

Work Timings: 5:30am to 2:30pm

About the role:

We're looking for a highly skilled and strategic Product Marketing Manager to join our team. In this pivotal role, you will be responsible for supporting local country teams with the successful launch of new products,.

You will serve as the central point of contact, coordinating across multiple marketing and cross-functional teams to ensure our marketing go-to-market strategies are executed flawlessly, on time, and with maximum impact. You will be part of a small team that drives strategic alignment, manages dependencies, and mitigates risks, turning complex cross-functional projects into streamlined, successful GTM launches.

What you will be doing:

  • Strategic Planning: Collaborate with stakeholders to define and document 360 GTM plans
  • Reporting: Lead Performance and Results Reporting process, coordinating across functions for inputs.
  • Program Management Leadership: Support the end-to-end GTM program management for product launches from initial planning to post-launch analysis.
  • Cross-Functional Coordination: Serve as a key POC liaison across a complex cross-functional team to ensure a unified and effective GTM strategy.
  • Risk & Dependency Management: Proactively identify potential risks, bottlenecks, and dependencies, and develop mitigation plans and operational systems to keep programs on track.
  • Communication: Establish and maintain clear communication channels, providing regular updates on program status, key milestones, and performance to all stakeholders.
  • Process Improvement: Continuously assess and refine processes and best practices to improve efficiency and effectiveness for future launches.

What you need to be great in this role:

  • Go to Market Strategic thinking: 9 to 12 years of experience in go-to-market strategies, ideally in-house. Demonstrated ability to understand business objectives and translate them into actionable GTM plans.
  • Program management : Proven ability to manage complex, cross-functional projects from inception to completion. Strong organizational skills, attention to detail
  • Data analysis: Strong analytical skills and able to put together analysis across varied data sets
  • Narrative building: Skilled at making presentation decks for storytelling
  • Mindset: highly proactive approach. Thrives in ambiguity
  • Collaboration: Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills. Ability to build strong relationships with teams in diverse cultures and locations, and influence stakeholders without direct authority.
  • Tools: Proficiency with project management software (e.g., Asana, Jira, Smartsheet), as well as GTM-related tools and platforms.
  • Passion for and inquisitive about AI and new technologies
  • Understanding and knowledge of AI tools is beneficial, but ability to learn and digest benefits and features of AI tools is critical.

title: Creative Director, Brand
description:
About Applied Intuition

Applied Intuition, Inc. is powering the future of physical AI. Founded in 2017 and now valued at $15 billion, the Silicon Valley company is creating the digital infrastructure needed to bring intelligence to every moving machine on the planet. Applied Intuition services the automotive, defense, trucking, construction, mining and agriculture industries in three core areas: tools and infrastructure, operating systems, and autonomy. Eighteen of the top 20 global automakers, as well as the United States military and its allies, trust the company’s solutions to deliver physical intelligence. Applied Intuition is headquartered in Sunnyvale, California, with offices in Washington, D.C.; San Diego; Ft. Walton Beach, Florida; Ann Arbor, Michigan; London; Stuttgart; Munich; Stockholm; Bangalore; Seoul; and Tokyo. Learn more at applied.co.

We are an in-office company, and our expectation is that employees primarily work from their Applied Intuition office 5 days a week. However, we also recognize the importance of flexibility and trust our employees to manage their schedules responsibly. This may include occasional remote work, starting the day with morning meetings from home before heading to the office, or leaving earlier when needed to accommodate family commitments.

About the role

As Design Director, Brand, you will be responsible for evolving and executing on Applied Intuition’s brand language across a wide range of touch points. From digital touch points like our website, products, and sales presentations to physical ones like a booth at CES or an entire conference event (eg. Physical AI Day and Intersect), you will define the creative direction that delights our audiences. You will collaborate closely with Product Design, Marketing, Production, Sales, Product and leadership across all verticals from automotive to construction, mining and defense.

At Applied Intuition, you will:

  • Collaborate with our Head of Design and Marketing and the other leaders on the team to build a bold and unique brand for Applied Intuition
  • Manage, lead and grow the brand design team
  • Conceptualize, design, and produce a wide range of marketing collateral, including campaigns, website, Swag store, print booklets, events, and more
  • Manage and elevate presentation resources for our global sales and product teams
  • Assist executive leadership with producing engaging communications for internal and external audiences (e.g. board meetings, customer on-sites etc.)
  • Contribute to the Design and Marketing leadership team

We're looking for someone who has:

  • 10+ years of experience driving visionary work in brand design and art direction.
  • Ability to think strategically and present work to the founders and other leaders
  • 5+ years of experience managing, leading and growing an exceptional design team
  • A portfolio that showcases world class design that up-levels the brand
  • Ability to collaborate with cross functional partners

Nice to have:

  • Prior work experience at a high-growth startup

Compensation at Applied Intuition for eligible roles includes base salary, equity, and benefits. Base salary is a single component of the total compensation package, which may also include equity in the form of options and/or restricted stock units, comprehensive health, dental, vision, life and disability insurance coverage, 401k retirement benefits with employer match, learning and wellness stipends, and paid time off. Note that benefits are subject to change and may vary based on jurisdiction of employment.

Applied Intuition pay ranges reflect the minimum and maximum intended target base salary for new hire salaries for the position. The actual base salary offered to a successful candidate will additionally be influenced by a variety of factors including experience, credentials & certifications, educational attainment, skill level requirements, interview performance, and the level and scope of the position.

Please reference the job posting’s subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the location listed is: $200,000 - $302,000 USD annually.

Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

Applied Intuition is an equal opportunity employer and federal contractor or subcontractor. However, we also recognize the importance of flexibility and trust our employees to manage their schedules responsibly. This may include occasional remote work, starting the day with morning meetings from home before heading to the office, or leaving earlier when needed to accommodate family commitments.

About the role

The Director of Strategic Growth serves as the senior relationship and shaping layer of our DoD business development operation. Reporting to the Head of Growth, you will be responsible for opening doors, building deep credibility with government stakeholders, and shaping programs well before they reach the public domain. You will operate as a high-level owner on a specific portfolio of domain accounts, partnering with a Capture Manager to convert strategic opportunities into a winning pipeline.

At Applied Intuition, you will:

  • Cultivate senior-level trust-based relationships with PEOs, PMs, requirements officers, and CTO offices.
  • Shape opportunities 12-18 months in advance by influencing requirements and acquisition strategies.
  • Lead strategic customer discovery to understand long-term roadmaps, budget trajectories, and political dynamics.
  • Define win strategies, identifying competitive discriminators and making critical 'pursue vs. walk away' decisions.
  • Provide strategic direction to the Capture Manager to ensure execution is aligned with shaping work.
  • Author white papers and lead capability demonstrations that position our technology in the customer's thinking.

We’re looking for someone who has:

  • BS or MS in Engineering, Political Science, National Security, or a related field.
  • 15–25 years of experience in or around the DoD (Military O-5/O-6, GS-15/SES, or senior Industry BD).
  • Expert-level knowledge of the PPBE cycle and the POM process.
  • Proven track record of shaping requirements for multi-million dollar defense programs.
  • Specific, 'warm' relationships within target commands (e.g., Army Futures Command, NAVSEA, or AFMC).
  • Experience operating in a "lean" environment, directly engaging in work rather than managing large support staffs.
  • Active Secret or Top Secret security clearance.

Nice to have:

  • Former experience as a Program Manager at DARPA, DIU, or a Service Research Lab.
  • Established network within relevant Congressional staff or Authorization/Appropriation committees.
  • Deep understanding of autonomous systems, modeling & simulation, or Vehicle OS architectures.

About the role

As a Marketing Manager, you’ll shape how the world understands our brand and products, driving Applied Intuition’s position as the leader in physical AI.

Bringing go-to-market experience, strategic rigor, creative judgement, and a curious mind, you'll help lead and execute marketing programs that drive business impact and build Applied Intuition's brand with customers, talent, investors, and press.

You'll join and learn from a fast-paced, high-performing team—marketers, designers, creative directors, writers, and producers— and work closely with engineers at the forefront of AI and the industries that move our world: automotive, trucking, mining, construction, defense, and agriculture.

At Applied Intuition, you will:

  • Support and lead marketing activities, collaborating closely across the marketing team, cross-functional stakeholders, and external customers and partners
  • Help define and shape how Applied Intuition shows up across industries, reinforcing our leadership in physical AI and connecting our story to broader customer and market needs
  • Work with internal writers, designers, and producers to develop and execute content across the full marketing mix — including multi-channel campaigns, video, blogs, press, social media, case studies, and events
  • Set campaign goals, analyze performance, and use insights to optimize future programs and drive measurable impact
  • Stay on top of market trends, industry dynamics, and competitor activity to inform strategy and maintain a differentiated brand perspective

We’re looking for someone who has:

  • 3+ years of tech marketing experience
  • Experience building and executing multi-channel marketing activities
  • Strong project management skills and the ability to run multiple workstreams effectively
  • Deep interest in AI and experience using AI tools in their work

title: Regional Marketing ManagerIPBS
description:
Reports to: Regional Head
Location: Remote

Level: Mid to Senior (4–7 years' relevant experience)

Working with: Regional BDM/PAM team, Brand & Creative, Performance, Compliance, Product

Industry: Multi-asset crypto and CFD trading

The role

As Regional Marketing Manager for IPBS, you own the marketing roadmap for one of the fastest-growing trading audiences in the world. You will translate global commercial goals into culturally fluent campaigns, drive qualified acquisition at a controlled CPA, and increase the LTV of active traders across the region. You will work hand-in-hand with our regional BDM/PAM team to amplify partner activity, and act as PrimeXBT's brand guardian on the ground.

This is a builder's role. You should be equally comfortable writing a brief for a creator campaign, negotiating booth space at a Karachi expo, optimising a paid social funnel for Tier-2 IPBS cities, and pulling AI tooling into your daily workflow to multiply your output.

Key responsibilities

Strategic planning & localisation

  • Own the annual IPBS marketing roadmap, aligned to global KPIs for acquisition, FTD and retention.
  • Run deep-dive market intelligence on local competitors (Delta Exchange, CoinDCX, Mudrex, Octa, Exness, OctaFX and equivalents), pricing models and trader behaviour to surface untapped segments.
  • Localise creative, messaging, landing pages and promotional offers so they land in Hindi, English, Urdu, Bengali and Sinhala/Tamil contextsnot just translated, but rewritten for the market.

Go-to-market strategy

  • Design and lead 360 regional campaigns spanning paid digital, organic, creator/KOL, PR, offline events and partner enablement.
  • Build quarterly GTM plans with clear hypotheses, channel mix, budget allocation and success metrics.
  • Partner with Product on regionally-relevant features, deposit methods and bonus mechanics.

Performance & growth marketing

  • Lead regional acquisition: act as a link with our Paid Media team in planning and rollout of PPC, Paid Social (Meta, X, YouTube), Programmatic and ASO campaigns specific to IPBS, with tight control over CPA and CPL.
  • Funnel optimisation: analyse the journey from first touch → registration → KYC → FTD → second deposit, and ship regional fixes (creative, landing page, payment UX, comms cadence) to reduce drop-off.
  • Retention & LTV: design lifecycle campaigns, reactivation flows and bonus/promotion mechanics that keep active traders trading.
  • Local partnerships: build co-marketing toolkits and campaign-in-a-box assets that the BDM/PAM team can deploy with IBs, affiliates and KOLs.

Agency & stakeholder management

  • Manage external media agencies, PR firms, creative shops and freelance localisers across the region.
  • Work cross-functionally with Compliance, Legal, Product and Sales to ship campaigns that are both impactful and legally sound non-negotiable in this region.
  • Brief, review and sign off all regional creative before it goes live.

Brand & offline presence

  • Events: plan and execute regional participation in major expos (Forex Expo Dubai, iFX EXPO Asia, Money Expo India, Traders Fair) and host VIP seminars and partner dinners for HNW clients and top affiliates.
  • Media buying: manage relationships with regional financial portals, business news outlets and trading-focused YouTubers/Telegram operators.
  • PR & communications: act as brand guardian, ensuring all PR activity respects local advertising rules around crypto and CFDs.

AI-augmented operating model

We expect every member of the marketing team to use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Midjourney, ElevenLabs, HeyGen and equivalents) as a force-multiplier for research, copy variants, creative iteration, competitor teardowns, ad concept testing, localised content production and reporting. Candidates who can demonstrate concrete examples of AI in their workflow will stand out.

What you'll bring

Must have

  • 4–7 years in performance/brand marketing, with at least 2 years owning a region or country P&L.
  • Deep familiarity with at least two IPBS marketsIndia is non-negotiable; Pakistan, Bangladesh or Sri Lanka exposure strongly preferred.
  • Track record running paid acquisition at scale across Meta, Google, programmatic and creator/KOL channels, with clear CPA/FTD numbers you can talk through.
  • Hands-on campaign craft: you can write a brief, review creative, read a media plan and challenge an agency without hiding behind one.
  • Working knowledge of crypto, forex, CFDs or another regulated financial vertical.
  • Fluent English plus at least one of: Hindi, Urdu, Bengali, Tamil or Sinhala.
  • Demonstrable AI-in-workflow examplesyou don't just talk about it, you ship with it.

Nice to have

  • Existing relationships with IPBS trading KOLs, financial media or expo organisers.
  • Experience working alongside an affiliate/IB programme, not just B2C performance.
  • SEO, lifecycle/CRM marketing, or community-building (Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp) chops.
  • Experience navigating local advertising restrictions on crypto or leveraged products.

How we measure success

  • Regional FTD volume and FTD CAC against quarterly targets.
  • Funnel conversi

title: Senior Manager, CRM Marketing, India & MENA
description:
About Crunchyroll

Founded by fans, Crunchyroll delivers the art and culture of anime to a passionate community. We super-serve over 100 million anime and manga fans across 200+ countries and territories, and help them connect with the stories and characters they crave. Whether that experience is online or in-person, streaming video, theatrical, games, merchandise, events and more, it’s powered by the anime content we all love.

Join our team, and help us shape the future of anime!

About the Role

Crunchyroll is seeking a Senior Manager, CRM Marketing to lead localized CRM engagement for India and MENA—two of our fastest-growing regions. Based in Mumbai or Hyderabad, this role will own the CRM campaign calendar for the region, balancing high-velocity title moments and catalog beats while bringing our biggest anime moments to life across owned channels.

You will plan and execute high-impact, fan-first campaigns that support title launches and catalog discovery—including new season/series drops, dub drops, mid-season arcs, and finales for top-tier titles. Success in this role requires strong segmentation and analytical judgment, plus the ability to translate IP into messaging that creates an emotional connection with fans.

You'll also act as a subject matter expert for India and MENA, championing transcreation and locally resonant storytelling (not just translation), cultural nuance, and fan-first engagement. You'll partner closely with our global Lifecycle Marketing team that owns always-on lifecycle journeys; in this role, you will serve as a regional advisor and ensure key title moments and local insights are reflected in the broader lifecycle strategy.

What You'll Do

  • CRM Campaign Calendar Ownership: Own the CRM campaign calendar for India and MENA across tentpole releases, seasonal beats, and catalog programming, aligning timing, targeting strategy, and channel plans with local and global priorities.
  • Go-to-Market Campaigns: Collaborate with regional marketing, brand, programming, and curation teams to design localized go-to-market moments for major content launches, seasonal events, partnerships, and fan activations.
  • Segmentation & Audience Strategy: Apply best practices in personalization and segmentation to build targeted campaigns informed by fan behavior, affinities, regional nuances, and language/dub preferences. Partner with Data Science and Growth teams to refine audience definitions, identify opportunities, and improve precision over time.
  • Regional Market Expertise: Serve as Crunchyroll's CRM market lead for India and MENA, advising on cultural nuances, local market behaviors, channel norms, and fan engagement opportunities.
  • Localized Lifecycle Strategy: Provide regional inputs into India and MENA lifecycle journey strategies across acquisition, onboarding, engagement, retention, and winback, working closely with our Lifecycle Marketing team. Ensure best practices in personalization, segmentation, and lifecycle orchestration.
  • Marketing Technology Ownership: Lead campaign execution and performance optimization in partnership with Operations, Martech, and Data Analytics; manage CRM initiatives within Braze (or similar) across email, push, in-app, notification center, and relevant messaging channels, ensuring excellence in configuration, QA, measurement, and reporting. Own content-triggered messaging strategy that supports title moments and engagement in alignment with lifecycle standards and guardrails.
  • Cross-Functional Partnership: Work with Regional Marketing, Product, Brand, Content, Creative, Data Science, and GTM teams to create cohesive fan experiences and ensure lifecycle campaigns align with both local and global priorities.
  • Performance & Optimization: Analyze CRM campaign performance, identify opportunities, and apply insights to continuously improve effectiveness and fan impact through test-and-learn approaches, including cohort analysis and incrementality-minded measurement.
  • Innovation & Experimentation: Stay ahead of market dynamics and messaging ecosystems (mobile-first behaviors, regional languages/Hinglish, WhatsApp norms, payment behaviors, and device/telco ecosystem nuances) to evolve CRM campaign strategy and execution.

About You

We get excited about candidates who bring:

  • 8+ years of experience in CRM, retention, or subscriber engagement marketing, ideally within streaming, digital subscription, gaming, e-commerce, or entertainment.
  • Strong experience with Braze (or comparable orchestration platforms) with a focus on campaign planning/execution, segmentation, triggers, and multi-channel delivery.
  • Deep knowledge of Indian and MENA consumer behaviors, digital marketing platforms, and messaging ecosystems (especially WhatsApp, UPI-driven funnels).
  • Strong creative acumen and storytelling ability—capable of translating IP into fan-first messaging that builds emotional connection and drives action.
  • Strong analytical skills to interpret performance data, develop insights, and translate them into targeting and creative optimizations.
  • Proven ability to collaborate across regional and global teams in a fast-paced, matrixed environment.
  • Passion for anime, entertainment, or fan-first brands is highly valued.
  • Additional proficiency in relevant regional languages is a plus.
  • Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, or related field.

About the Team

Our Lifecycle Marketing team designs personalized journeys that drive fan connection, engagement,

title: GTM & Partner Marketing, Interactive Experiences, Senior Manager
description:
About Crunchyroll

Founded by fans, Crunchyroll delivers the art and culture of anime to a passionate community. We super-serve over 100 million anime and manga fans across 200+ countries and territories, and help them connect with the stories and characters they crave. Whether that experience is online or in-person, streaming video, theatrical, games, merchandise, events and more, it’s powered by the anime content we all love.

Join our team, and help us shape the future of anime!

About the role

We're looking for a Senior Manager to lead go-to-market (GTM) strategy and execution for our interactive experiences, including mobile games, metaverse projects, and unique co-marketing campaigns with major publishers and partners.

Reporting to the VP, GTM & Partnership Marketing, you'll design and launch programs for interactive games and experiences, creating campaigns that delight fans, drive subscriber growth, and adapt to local markets around the world.

Core Responsibilities

  • Lead GTM and partner marketing for interactive projects—from defining goals and audiences to creative direction and performance metrics.
  • Build and share full-funnel plans that cover launches, app store promotions, partner activations, and post-launch optimization.
  • Be the main point of contact across leadership, other teams, and partners to ensure agreement and smooth execution.
  • Partner with marketing, product, and lifecycle teams to create custom campaigns that lift acquisition and engagement.
  • Define KPIs and partner with analytics to measure performance and guide optimization.
  • Forecast impact and build playbooks to scale successful initiatives across markets.
  • Turn performance data into clear next steps—creating presentations and updates for executives and teams.
  • Adapt global strategies to local markets, using regional insights, cultural moments, and platform trends to drive results.

We are considering applicants available to work a hybrid schedule, with three days each week onsite at our Los Angeles office.

About You

We get excited about candidates, like you, because you bring hands-on experience leading GTM and partner marketing for interactive games and platforms, turning launches into measurable fan engagement and growth.

  • 8+ years experience in GTM, partnership, or product marketing within mobile games, interactive platforms, entertainment, or consumer tech, with ownership of end-to-end launches and live operations
  • Proven track record launching interactive GTM with AAA/platform partners—comfortable moving from strategy to execution with clear frameworks, briefs, timelines, and high-quality delivery
  • Experienced across the full growth stack: channels, partner activations, lifecycle, promotions, product, app stores, and interactive marketplaces
  • Data-driven problem solver with strong analytics skills to assess performance, identify opportunities, and improve outcomes
  • Global experience across the US, Europe, LATAM, India, Southeast Asia, and the Middle East
  • You have a Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience in GTM and partner marketing

About the Team

Both domestic and ever-expanding international efforts fuel Crunchyroll's global expansion. The GTM & Planning team collaborates with U.S.-based leadership and regional teams to develop go-to-market and partnership marketing plans and programs, expanding Crunchyroll's growth through its own operations (O&O) presence and high-profile third-party distribution partners. This approach delights and super-serves anime fans, boosts its footprint and brand awareness, and makes Crunchyroll synonymous with anime worldwide. The GTM & Planning team is part of the Growth & Planning team, a performance marketing organization.

Why you will love working at Crunchyroll

In addition to getting to work with fun, passionate and inspired colleagues, you will also enjoy the following benefits and perks:

  • Receive a great compensation package including salary plus performance bonus earning potential, paid annually.
  • Flexible time off policies allowing you to take the time you need to be your whole self.
  • Generous medical, dental, vision, STD, LTD, and life insurance
  • Health Saving Account HSA program
  • Health care and dependent care FSA
  • 401(k) plan, with employer match
  • Employer paid commuter benefit
  • Support program for new parents
  • Pet insurance and some of our offices are pet friendly!

#LifeAtCrunchyroll #LI-Hybrid

The range listed is just one component of Crunchyroll’s Total Rewards offerings for employees. Other rewards may include performance bonuses, employer matched retirement savings, time-off programs, and progressive health benefits and perks.

$115,000—$142,000 USD

We want to be everything for someone rather than something for everyone and we do this by living and modeling our values in all that we do. We value

title: Senior Director, Consumer Marketing
location: New York City, New York, United States
description:
WHAT WE DO MATTERS:

At The Knot Worldwide, we champion celebration - and that starts with celebrating our people. Our employees are passionate dreamers, thoughtful doers, and lifelong learners who power meaningful moments for millions around the world. We’re united by authentic connection, shared purpose, and a deep commitment to the global community we serve. Here, flexibility and belonging go hand in hand with high performance. Driven by our core values, we believe the best ideas come from empowered teams: those who consistently collaborate with intention to design solutions, spark ideas, and drive impact. Our people are at the heart of our success.

ABOUT THE ROLE AND OUR TEAM:

The Senior Director, Consumer Marketing is a senior marketing leader responsible for developing and leading the integrated marketing strategy across The Knot’s U.S. consumer brands and products. This role drives full-funnel marketing programs that build brand affinity, acquire and engage couples throughout their planning journey, and accelerate growth across The Knot’s ecosystem.

This leader will bring together brand storytelling, data-driven growth marketing, and consumer lifecycle expertise to deliver cohesive, high-impact marketing programs across paid, owned, earned, and partner channels. The Senior Director will play a critical role in identifying the highest-impact opportunities for the business, prioritizing initiatives across channels and teams, and ensuring marketing investments drive both short-term performance and long-term brand equity.

Partnering closely with marketing leadership and cross-functional partners across product, brand, insights, and channel teams, this leader will orchestrate integrated campaigns and programs that reflect a deep understanding of the wedding planning journey and evolving consumer needs.

The ideal candidate is a whole-brained marketer who understands how brand and performance work together across the full marketing funnel. They bring clarity to complex business problems and provide simple, easy-to-comprehend frameworks. They bring a strong track record of building high-performing teams, driving cross-functional alignment, and delivering measurable business impact through integrated marketing strategies.

The annual salary range for this position is $151,563-$259,822. Starting pay is determined based on a combination of factors which may include location/market, education, experience, skills, and other qualifications of the successful candidate. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, commissions, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. We also offer benefits to eligible employees including medical, dental, retirement, and generous leave policies.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
  • Lead Consumer Marketing Strategy: Own the integrated consumer marketing strategy across The Knot’s U.S. brands and products, ensuring cohesive programs that drive awareness, engagement, and growth across the full marketing funnel.
  • Build and Lead a High-Performing Team: Lead, mentor, and scale a team of marketers responsible for integrated campaign strategy and execution. Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and creative excellence while empowering the team to deliver high-impact results.
  • Drive Integrated Marketing Programs: Develop and oversee cross-channel marketing programs that bring together brand storytelling and performance marketing to drive measurable business outcomes. Ensure campaigns are coordinated across paid, owned, and earned channels to maximize reach and impact.
  • Set Strategic Priorities Across Initiatives: Identify and prioritize the highest-impact marketing initiatives across channels, teams, and business objectives. Ensure resources and investments are focused on the programs that will drive the greatest impact for the U.S. brands.
  • Develop the Consumer Marketing Calendar: Lead the planning and execution of the annual consumer marketing calendar across US brands, aligning campaigns and initiatives to key consumer moments, seasonality, and business priorities.
  • Drive Data-Informed Decision Making: Establish reporting rigor, dashboards, and leverage consumer insights, competitive intelligence, analytics, and experimentation to continuously refine marketing strategies, messaging, and channel mix to improve effectiveness and efficiency.
  • Lead Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with Product, Business Managers, and Marketing leadership (Product Marketing, Creative, CRM, Media, and Insights teams) to develop cohesive campaigns and product GTMs to ensure a seamless consumer experience that improves product adoption.
  • Ensure Strong Brand & Product Positioning: Guide the development of messaging strategies that clearly communicate The Knot’s value propositions to couples while strengthening brand differentiation and relevance in the market.
  • Optimize Marketing Investment and Impact: Evaluate program performance and marketing investment effectiveness to ensure initiatives deliver measurable impact and support sustainable growth for the business.
  • Executive Communication & Influence: Present marketing strategies, campaign performance, and strategic recommendations to senior leadership, influencing key decisions and aligning stakeholders around shared priorities.

title: Senior Marketing Operations Manager
description:
About AlphaSense:

The world’s most sophisticated companies rely on AlphaSense to remove uncertainty from decision-making. With market intelligence and search built on proven AI, AlphaSense delivers insights that matter from content you can trust. Our universe of public and private content includes equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, trade journals, and clients’ own research content.

The acquisition of Tegus by AlphaSense in 2024 advances our shared mission to empower professionals to make smarter decisions through AI-driven market intelligence. Together, AlphaSense and Tegus will accelerate growth, innovation, and content expansion, with complementary product and content capabilities that enable users to unearth even more comprehensive insights from thousands of content sets. Our platform is trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500. Founded in 2011, AlphaSense is headquartered in New York City with more than 2,000 employees across the globe and offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland. Come join us!

Location: Remote (preferably Eastern time zone)

Reports to: Senior Director, Marketing Operations


About the Team:

The Marketing Operations team enables marketing performance by transforming strategy into execution through data, systems, automation and operational excellence.

About the Role:

We are seeking a highly strategic and operationally minded Marketing Operations Manager focused on marketing data infrastructure, systems integrations and scalable operational architecture. This role will serve as a key partner across Marketing, RevOps, IT and Security to build and maintain the data foundations that power our go-to-market organization.

This individual will own the design, optimization, governance and operational reliability of marketing data flows and integrations across our marketing technology ecosystem. The ideal candidate combines strong technical and analytical capabilities with exceptional cross-functional collaboration, documentation and process management skills.

What You’ll Do:

  • Marketing Data Infrastructure & Integrations
    • Define, build and maintain scalable data flows and integrations across marketing systems to ensure reliable, consistent and accessible data
    • Design and document integration architecture, field mappings, transformation logic, sync behavior and system dependencies
    • Monitor and troubleshoot integration failures, synchronization issues and operational bottlenecks
    • Partner with RevOps and IT to implement and optimize cross-functional integrations, APIs and automation workflows
    • Support onboarding and integration of new marketing technologies and vendors
  • Governance, Scalability & Operational Excellence
    • Develop and maintain documentation for system architecture, workflows, governance standards and operational procedures
    • Help establish scalable standards for system configuration, integration design, change management and operational support
    • Support data governance, compliance, audit and security initiatives in partnership with internal stakeholders
    • Identify opportunities to improve data quality, reduce manual operational work and increase process scalability through automation
  • Cross-Functional Data Enablement
    • Partner with Marketing Data & Analytics, RevOps and business stakeholders to support trusted reporting, attribution and funnel visibility
    • Support scalable reporting datasets, operational dashboards and data accessibility across systems

Who You Are:

  • 5–8 years of experience in Marketing Operations, Revenue Operations, Marketing Technology or related operational roles
  • Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
  • Strong understanding of marketing automation platforms, CRM integrations, lead lifecycle management and marketing data architecture
  • Experience working with APIs, integration platforms, workflow automation tools or cross-system data flows
  • Strong analytical, troubleshooting and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail
  • Experience partnering cross-functionally with Marketing, RevOps, IT, Analytics and other technical/non-technical stakeholders
  • Comfortable operating in fast-paced, high-growth environments with evolving processes and priorities
  • Strong documentation, organization and operational process management skills
  • Experience supporting data governance, compliance or audit-related initiatives is a plus

For base compensation, we set standard ranges for all roles based on function and level benchmarked against similar stage growth companies and internal comparables. In order to be compliant with local legislation, as well as to provide greater transparency to candidates, we share salary ranges on all job postings regardless of desired hiring location. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including candidate experience/expertise and may vary from the amounts listed below.

You may also be offered a performance-based bonus, equity, and a generous benefits program.

Base Compensation Range
$114,000—$140,000

  • Lead product launches for infrastructure and security-centric releases. Partner with Product and Engineering to ensure launches include the necessary technical content (e.g. technical FAQs, architecture diagrams, and security white papers) required for market readiness.

  • Data-driven and analytically minded, leveraging product adoption data, competitive intelligence, and technical ROI metrics to identify market opportunities and inform the product roadmap.
  • Excellent written and verbal communicator with experience delivering technical dem

title: Founding Director of Product Marketing
description:
About the job

Role: Founding Director of Product Marketing

Location: Remote, anywhere in the United States
Team: Marketing
Reports to: VP of Marketing
Stage: Series A, B2B SaaS


The Company

Enterpret is redefining how businesses understand and act on customer feedback. We’re building an AI-native platform that centralizes feedback from every source - surveys, reviews, support tickets, communities and turns it into clear, actionable insights that drive business growth.

We’re trusted by some of the world’s most customer-obsessed companies from Canva, Notion and Perplexity, and backed by top-tier investors like Kleiner Perkins, Canaan Partners and Peak XV Partners (previously known as Sequoia India). Our mission: to unlock the voice of the customer for every product team on the planet.

The Role

We’re looking for a Founding Director of Product Marketing who can turn strategic narrative into market momentum. You’ll work side by side with the VP of Marketing to shape Enterpret’s category story and bring it to life across launches, campaigns and enablement.

Your focus is to bridge strategy and execution by taking positioning, insights, and vision and translating them into messaging, content, and GTM programs that drive awareness, credibility and demand.

You’ll partner closely with the Growth Lead to fuel the inbound engine - ensuring every launch, message and piece of content aligns to how buyers search, learn, and convert.

This is a true 0→1 role. Our ICP, positioning, and category narrative are still being sharpened. We’re looking for someone who has done this work at an early-stage company and can help us build the foundation (and decision rules) that the entire business can scale on. This role is heavily enterprise-oriented, with complex sales cycles where sales enablement and category education matter.

This role is also for someone who wants to challenge their skills, who loves making ideas concrete and wants to leave fingerprints on how Enterpret becomes the category leader in this space.

Responsibilities

In this position, you will:

  • Fuel inbound growth: Work with Growth to turn insights, launches, and thought leadership into campaigns, content, and programs that generate organic and earned demand. Shift our inbound engine from primarily competitive capture to category education, so buyers discover Enterpret earlier and make us their first call.

  • Bring the category to life: Turn Enterpret’s vision into clear, differentiated stories that resonate across every market touchpoint. Internally, define and enforce decision rules (positioning, ICP, category POV) so the company speaks with one voice across Marketing, Sales, Product, and CS.

  • Create a small set of marquee “category education” assets early (e.g., POV narrative, category guide, competitive story, proof-based messaging) and build a scalable content engine from there.

  • Execute GTM launches: Lead product and feature launches end-to-end from messaging and positioning to campaign rollout and internal enablement.

  • Build enterprise-ready sales enablement: talk tracks, objection handling, competitive narratives, proof points, and discovery frameworks.

  • Enable the business: Work with Sales, Product and CS teams to translate insights and proof points into compelling, market-facing stories. Partner closely with our Head of Customer Intelligence.

  • Measure and iterate: Use data and feedback to refine messaging, campaign assets and positioning over time. Define what “winning” looks like early (e.g., improvements in inbound quality, conversion rates, sales cycle confidence, and category signal) and instrument feedback loops with Growth + Sales.

Essential Requirements

  • 8+ years of B2B SaaS product marketing experience, with at least one early-stage (Series A–B) “0→1” experience where you helped define positioning/ICP and built core GTM foundations.

  • Proven experience executing GTM programs and launches that drive tangible market impact.

  • Exceptional storytelling and copywriting skills - you can make complex ideas simple and compelling.

  • Comfort in turning strategy into deliverables from decks and sales assets to blog outlines and video briefs.

  • Strong partnership skills where you thrive collaborating with Growth, Product, and Sales.

  • A builder’s mindset: you care about craft and creating things that last.

  • A bias for action: you build, test, learn, and iterate with speed.

  • Experience supporting enterprise sales motions (complex cycles, higher ACVs, deal-by-deal enablement).

  • Comfort operating in ambiguity: you can diagnose what’s unclear, make decisions with imperfect info, and bring others along.

  • Excitement to build from zero, collaborate closely with leadership, and push the boundaries of what growth looks like at Series A.

  • Able to commit to 3 days in person in office, situated in Union Square, Manhattan, New York City.


Why You'll Love This

  • You’ll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the VP of Marketing to bring the category to life.
  • You’ll own high-impact decisions across channels and strategy, not just execution.
  • You’ll move fast in a low-ego, no-BS team that values speed, data, and creativity.
  • You'll work alongside other A+ talent who are both brilliant and care a lot.
  • You’ll see your work directly influence how an industry disc

title: Marketing Manager (Gujarat & Rajasthan )
description:
About Monster Energy:

Forget about blending in. That's not our style. We're the risk-takers, the trailblazers, the game-changers. We're not perfect, and we don't pretend to be. We're raw, unfiltered, and a bit unconventional. But our drive is unrivaled, just like our athletes.   • Additional Experience Desired: Between 3-5 years of experience in marketing coordination, trade marketing, and event execution.
  • Computer Skills Desired: Proficient in using Microsoft Office (Words, PowerPoint, Excel).

title: Senior Lifecycle Growth Manager
description:
About Ethos

Ethos is a leading life insurance technology company on a mission to protect families by democratizing access to life insurance and empowering agents at scale. With its robust three-sided technology platform, Ethos is transforming the life insurance experience for consumers, agents, and carriers alike. Ethos offers instant, accessible products and a seamless online process that requires no medical exams and just a few health questions; it eliminates traditional barriers, making it easier than ever for everyone to protect their families. Ethos is redefining how life insurance is bought, sold, and underwritten.

About the role:

We are hiring a Sr. Lifecycle Growth Manager to own activation end-to-end — the single highest-leverage stretch of our funnel. This is the work of recovering applicants who stall mid-application and converting approved customers who have not yet activated into paying policyholders. It is where intent either converts into revenue or leaks out, and it is the workstream with the most room to move the business in the next four quarters.

You will own the lifecycle strategy across two connected phases. In pre-approval, you will own the recovery layer for application drop-off — the sequences, nudges, and in-product interventions that bring stalled applicants back into the flow and through to approval. In ANA, you will own a fully personalized program segmented by where the approved customer dropped off and what the behavioral signals tell us about why.

This is a high-leverage IC role for someone who operates like a PM, thinks like a data scientist, and ships like an operator. You will run a high-velocity experimentation program, design product-led growth loops that go beyond email, and be the operational spine that turns activation OKRs into shipped work.

This is not a campaign-manager role. It is a role for someone who wants a seat at the strategy table, who can debate tradeoffs with a Director, and who is as comfortable writing SQL as writing a test plan.

What You'll Own:

Application Drop-Off Recovery

Own the lifecycle layer that recovers applicants who stall mid-application. You will map where drop-off concentrates across the application flow — saved-progress abandonment, step-level friction, medical exam scheduling hesitation, underwriting-window stalls — and build targeted recovery interventions for each leak point. You will work step-by-step, not in generic sequences: a customer who drops off at health disclosure gets a different treatment than one who drops off at payment details.

You will partner with Product and Engineering on in-product recovery surfaces (saved-progress resumption, step-specific support prompts) and own the outbound recovery motion across email, SMS, push, and Regal. Success is measured in step-to-step conversion lift and applicant-to-approved rate.

ANA Personalization

Own the ANA program with personalization as the core design principle, not a phase-two enhancement. The primary segmentation axis is drop-off point — a customer who was approved but never returned to view their quote needs a fundamentally different treatment than one who viewed a quote, selected a term, and abandoned at checkout. You will build distinct journeys for each drop-off cohort and layer secondary personalization on top: persona signals (price-sensitive, health-uncertain, comparison shopper, distracted/busy), term-length selected, quoted price band, and engagement recency.

You will own the evergreen ANA flow, term-length education moments, pricing transparency touchpoints, and social proof frameworks — all tuned to the specific drop-off point they are recovering. Success is measured in activation rate lift by cohort, not in email opens or aggregate sends.

Experimentation Engine

Own the end-to-end testing roadmap for activation. Move us from sporadic tests to a high-velocity program — 10+ well-designed experiments per quarter with clean hypotheses, appropriate power, and ruthless post-mortems. You decide what gets tested, you enforce statistical integrity, and you translate winners into roadmap changes. Incrementality tests, holdouts, frequency-cap experiments, and multivariate designs are in your default toolkit.

Product-Led Activation Growth

Activation is not an email problem. You will partner with Product and Engineering to design in-product interventions — application-step nudges, saved-progress recovery surfaces,

title: Senior Marketing Operations Manager

About the Role

We’re looking for an experienced Senior Marketing Operations Manager to oversee the delivery and analysis of Ethos’ CRM lifecycle campaigns, with an emphasis on email and SMS campaign execution. The ideal candidate is a thought partner for innovative use of experimentation, technology, data, and insights to drive campaign efficiency and effectiveness. You will also play a role in ensuring our Marketing/CRM capabilities are continuously evolving to meet the ongoing needs of the business and our customers. The position will work closely with all parts of the business to execute CRM deployment strategies that move business metrics.

This is a technical role that requires a blend of hands-on execution and longer-term, strategic planning. At Ethos, our offline experiences (email, SMS, direct mail, interactions with insurance agents) are all valuable touchpoints that we consider to be part of the core user experience. We’re looking for someone who is a consumer-first expert in email and SMS deployment strategy with meticulous attention to detail. This role will report into Ethos’ head of Partnership Lifecycle Marketing.

Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Strong collaboration skills in team environments, ability to effectively work with key stakeholders to clarify campaign requirements, but able to act quickly as the decision-maker
  • High technical aptitude with the ability to quickly ramp up on internal nuances related to people, processes, and technology.

  • Working familiarity with HTML/CSS
  • Comfort with data pipelines and architecture, targeting, multi-step workflows, QA, and reporting

  • Knowledge of the interconnectivity between technology and customer experience
  • Ability to compile a well-articulated dashboard and share performance metrics
  • Excellent project management skills, ability to multitask effectively, and work cross-functionally

title: Product Marketing Manager
description:
Role: Product Marketing Manager

Location: Mumbai

What you’ll do

We’re MiQ, a global programmatic media partner for marketers and agencies. Our people are at the heart of everything we do, so you will be too. No matter the role or the location, we’re all united in the vision to lead the programmatic industry and make it better.

As a Product Marketing Manager in our India Commercial department, you’ll have the chance to:

  • Delivery
    • Identify valuable target audiences and refine target personas by product as needed

    • Build compelling sales collateral and act as the main stakeholder for all sales materials delivered to the sales team

  • Project Management
    • Facilitate and improve internal product communication process and ensure alignment across sales, product and marketing departments
    • Own the go-to-market product release calendar and ensure deliverables are executed in line with these timelines

    • Develop a process for reporting on competitive industry intelligence
    • Acting with curiosity. Finds new and better ways of working, then applies or implements solutions to unarticulated needs, new or existing market requirements. Open to receiving and trying new ideas, processes or ways of working.

Who are your stakeholders?

  • Your key stakeholders will be on the product and commercial teams. You will lead the product commercialization charge across our suite of programmatic solutions including (but not limited to): Advanced TV, Total Video and MiQ Sigma.


What you’ll bring

Project Management

  • Highly organized and can demonstrate ability to plan and execute complex projects with multiple objectives and deliverables.

Analyzing

Product Storytelling:

Collaboration:

  • Proactively open and transparent on work priorities and progress with all stakeholders.

Communication:

  • Expert at making complex product information easy to understand.
  • Flawless command of the English language; written communication is always compelling, mistake free and in MiQ’s Tone of Voice.

YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

  • 5+ years of experience in the programmatic/digital advertising space
  • You have an understanding of agency and marketer needs
  • You have experience in product marketing, especially within adtech
  • You have an intimate understanding of the sales process for a managed service media business, including selling to both media agencies and media teams at brands directly
  • Excellent persuasive communicator in both written and verbal forms, adept at crafting compelling arguments
  • Creative thinker who can relate

Support the APAC Marketing team by improving operational processes and ensuring effective marketing initiatives. Responsibilities include:

  • Campaign Project Leadership: Lead the project execution of complex marketing campaigns, defining scope, managing timelines, coordinating cross-functional resources, and ensuring timely, on-budget delivery in partnership with the Product Marketing Manager (PMM). Oversee the optimization of DM, Channel, and In-game tools for maximum campaign effectiveness.
  • Performance Management & Insight: Own the tracking and reporting of departmental Objectives and Key Results (OKRs) and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on a weekly, monthly, and quarterly basis across key marketing functions. Analyze performance data to proactively identify bottlenecks, and implement data-driven solutions to drive ambitious growth targets.
  • Enablement & Governance: Develop and deliver comprehensive training and support on new MarTech tools and process best practices. Manage the new-hire onboarding process and establish the IP (Intellectual Property) usage guidelines and 'dos and don'ts' playbook for the regional team in collaboration with cross-functional partners.
  • Financial & Legal Oversight: Oversee and manage the operational aspects of the marketing budget, including tracking purchase orders, processing invoices, and maintaining vendor contracts to ensure financial compliance and legal adherence.
  • Stakeholder Management & Alignment: Serve as the primary liaison between APAC Marketing, Global teams, and key cross-functional stakeholders (e.g., Finance, Legal, Engineering) to drive process improvements and ensure strategic alignment and efficient project execution.

What we’re looking for

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related analytical field
  • 5-6+ years of progressive experience in Marketing Operations, Project Management, or a combined MOps/PM role, ideally within a high-growth technology company
  • Demonstrated ability to leverage strong analytical and problem-solving skills to diagnose workflow issues and implement scalable, data-driven solutions
  • Expertise in advanced data analysis, reporting, and data visualization (e.g., Tableau, Looker) to translate complex performance metrics into actionable business insights.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with a proven ability to organize, structure, and articulate complex information to diverse stakeholders
  • Proven ability to take proactive ownership of problems and processes, driving continuous improvement without constant oversight
  • Expert ability to prioritize and manage a high-volume portfolio of simultaneous projects, ensuring successful and timely delivery of each
  • Meticulous attention to detail and unwavering commitment to data accuracy and hygiene across the marketing technology stack
  • Deep understanding of the digital marketing funnel, channel performance, and core marketing metrics (e.g., CAC, LTV, Conversion Rates)
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, hybrid work environment, effectively managing pressure and rapid organizational change
  • Experience applying formal Project Management methodologies (e.g., Asana, Jira) to Marketing/Field Operations and leveraging AI/Automation tools (e.g., Codex, Python) to drive operational efficiency within the technology or gaming industry is a significant advantage.
  • Experience with field marketing operations/PM, particularly in the tech industry, is a Plus
  • Familiarity and genuine interest in the company's Intellectual Property (IP) and brand standards is a plus

We are committed to creating a diverse, supportive work environment where everyone is treated with respect. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us & embrace the adventure!

title: Principal Partner Marketing Manager
description:
A career that’s the whole package!

At Conga, we’ve built a community where our colleagues can thrive. Here you’ll find opportunities to innovate and support growth through individual and team development, all within an environment where every voice is heard.

Conga lines up commercial operations so companies run as connected, smarter businesses. By unifying the people and processes that drive commerce, Conga aligns pricing, quoting, contracting, rebates, and communications so teams stay in sync and buyers keep moving forward. The result is trusted decisions, consistent buyer experiences, and profitable growth. More than 10,000 customers worldwide, including over 50% of the Fortune 100®, trust Conga when commercial complexity is high and global impact is on the line.

Our approach is grounded in the Conga Way, a framework that reflects our values and drives everything from hiring to decision-making, as well as key programs including recognition. Created with direct input from our colleagues, the Conga Way forms the foundation of our vibrant culture.

Job Title: Principal Partner Marketing Manager

Locations: Bangalore / Ahmedabad

Reports to: Director, Global Partner Marketing

A Quick Snapshot

Conga is seeking a talented APAC Partner Marketing Manager to join our passionate and dynamic team of partner marketers. This key role will see you work cross-functionally with a variety of teams to build and scale marketing campaigns and activities with our largest global partners, and crucial regional partners. In this role, you will be responsible for defining, creating, executing and bringing go-to-market (GTM) strategies to life as you build deep relationships across our partners.

Why It's a Big Deal

As Principal Partner Marketing Manager, you will play a crucial role in defining the GTM strategies and building a deep relationship with our partners.

Are You the Person We're Looking For?

Experience. You must have more than 12 years of experience in Partner Marketing enablement within B2B software space.

Marketing Activities. You are excellent in creating and executing marketing activities such as webinars and other online and in-person event activities, to support integrated campaigns. You craft and develop marketing content for compelling joint solution offerings with strategic partner accounts including creation of required marketing assets (product videos, customer success stories, demos, presentations, how-to guides etc.).

GSI/RSI partnership. You know how to help engagement teams understand the value proposition of the Conga-GSI/RSI partnership through a multichannel approach that encompasses digital, events, training, enablement and more!

Conga Customer Marketing team, to develop impactful joint partner customer stories to help promote the partnership’s value to the market.

Conga Marketing Operations, to measure and report on pipeline creation, conversion, and ROI.

Self-Starter and motivated. You are Hard-working, self-starter with a willingness and eagerness to learn. You are organized, ambitious, and high-energy that can embrace and navigate a fast-paced, ever shifting environment.

Education. A bachelor’s degree in engineering or equivalent

Here's What Will Give You an Edge

Focus. You are Laser-focused on driving marketing activities that convert to revenue in a repeatable and scalable way.

Analytical. You are analytical, with the ability to capture and share key data and insights and use data to drive key marketing strategies and decisions.

Branding. You are influential in telling the Organizations story to all levels– from executives to the sales teams that are engaged in accounts. You have good understanding of enablement strategies and channels to get the brand and message embedded in the partners’ ecosystem. You demonstrate experience working with large partners on branding and demand generation initiatives.

Creative and innovative. You love thinking about the whole marketing funnel and making decisions based on data by being creative and innovative.

Relationship. You are excited to hit the ground running and use your experience to get to know and build successful long-term relationships with partner colleagues.
